g.text: Contract Tests — The Missing Microservices Layer
Same change: provider renames JSON field `user_id` → `userId`. With a contract test, caller's expectation kills the provider's PR. Without, it explodes downstream.
Provider team B renames JSON field: `user_id` → `userId`
Same change. Two outcomes. With contract: $10 PR fix. Without: $10,000+ prod incident — exactly the cost-of-defect ratio from §1.
Contract tests are the only cheap mechanism that catches cross-service breakage at PR time. Integration tests can't (each side mocks the other); E2E can't run fast enough on every PR.
